cases.title: Find stale README guidance after a repo change
cases.user_request: Audit the repo docs against this recent code or config change and tell me what is stale.

notes: A recent change touched a workflow, command, or interface that should be reflected in human-facing docs.
The response should focus on the doc mismatch, not rewrite the whole README.

static_checks: Names the code or workflow surface before naming the stale doc.
review_questions: Does the report tie the stale guidance to a concrete repo change?
Does it keep the fix narrowly scoped to the affected doc surface?
Would the user understand exactly what changed and why the doc is stale?

cases.title: Audit operational docs after a workflow or deployment change
cases.user_request: Check whether the operational docs and runbooks still match the current workflow after this change.

notes: The inspected change touches an operational workflow, script, or deployment surface.
The most important docs may be a runbook, playbook, or setup guide rather than a README.

static_checks: Focuses on operational accuracy rather than style-only improvements.
review_questions: Does the response include the docs that matter operationally, not just the most visible ones?
Does it avoid broad rewrite advice when a smaller fix would restore accuracy?
Would the result help someone operate or support the repo safely?

cases.title: Stay honest when docs look stale but the evidence is weak
cases.user_request: Audit the docs drift for this repo, but only report concrete mismatches; call out anything uncertain.

notes: The repository may have docs that look suspicious but lack a clear matching code change.
The response should distinguish confirmed drift from unverified suspicion.

artifact_requirements: confirmed findings or explicit no-drift result
static_checks: Does not manufacture drift findings without a concrete mismatch.
review_questions: Does the response clearly separate confirmed drift from suspicion?
Does it avoid turning uncertain issues into a concrete edit queue?
Would the user know whether any action is actually justified?
